task_completion.description: Create a password-protected page titled 'Staff Resources Portal' with specific content
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a page titled 'Staff Resources Portal' in the WordPress Pages list or editor. Verify that its visibility is set to 'Password Protected' (ideally showing the password 'LibAccess2024!' in the editor settings) and the content includes 'Welcome to the Library Staff Resources Portal'.

task_completion.description: Change visibility of 'Getting Started with WordPress' post to Private
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the post 'Getting Started with WordPress' in the WordPress Posts list and verify it has a 'Private' label next to its title, or check the post editor to see its visibility is set to 'Private'.

task_completion.description: Update status of '10 Essential WordPress Plugins Every Site Needs' post to Draft
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the post '10 Essential WordPress Plugins Every Site Needs' in the WordPress Posts list and verify it has a 'Draft' label next to its title, or check the post editor to see it is saved as a Draft.

task_completion.description: Create a private page titled 'Internal: Collection Development Policy' with specific content
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a page titled 'Internal: Collection Development Policy' in the WordPress Pages list with a 'Private' label, or in the editor with visibility set to 'Private'. The content must include the word 'deaccessioning'.

task_completion.description: Create a public page titled 'Accessing Restricted Resources' with specific content
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a page titled 'Accessing Restricted Resources' in the WordPress Pages list showing as published (without a Draft or Private label), or in the editor showing as Published. The content must include 'Contact the library director'.

integrity.description: Agent used the WordPress GUI in a web browser to make changes
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the WordPress admin dashboard in a web browser, not terminal commands like WP-CLI.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual WordPress application
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show genuine WordPress interfaces (Pages, Posts, Block Editor) rather than a text editor or mockups.

integrity.description: Agent did not copy-paste expected answers into a text editor
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no screenshots of the agent simply writing the expected titles, statuses, or content into a text editor or terminal without applying them in WordPress.

privileged_info_for_vlm:
1. 'Staff Resources Portal' page must be password-protected with the password 'LibAccess2024!' and content containing 'Welcome to the Library Staff Resources Portal'.
2. 'Getting Started with WordPress' post must have 'Private' visibility.
3. '10 Essential WordPress Plugins Every Site Needs' post must have 'Draft' status.
4. 'Internal: Collection Development Policy' page must have 'Private' visibility and content containing 'deaccessioning'.
5. 'Accessing Restricted Resources' page must be published (public) and content containing 'Contact the library director'.
